reasoning: Mauritius is an exceptionally stable democracy with no military and no recorded history or presence of organized armed groups (insurgencies, paramilitaries, or cartels). ACLED defines 'Battles' strictly as violent interactions between two organized armed groups. Given that the 30-day historical average leading up to April 2025 is zero, the resolution criteria simply asks whether at least 1 battle will occur within the 30-day window preceding each date. Because 23 days of the window for the first date (May 4, 2025) have already passed with no reports of armed conflict, and because this reference class of events essentially never happens in Mauritius, the probability of a battle occurring in any of these upcoming 1-month periods is near zero. I am placing my estimates at or near the lowest allowable floor of 0.05, with marginally increasing probabilities further out in time to reflect higher baseline uncertainty for distant horizons.
